WHEN INFLATION THREATENS DISCIPLINE, NOT FATE

The Reserve Bank has consistently shown that sustainable price stability requires distinguishing transitory supply shocks from entrenched inflationary pressures. If rates are raised too soon, it could dampen investment and consumption just when the economy’s expansion still needs accommodative financial conditions.

BHARAT RECLAIMS ITSELF

The question that contemporary India faces is not whether or not it should become modern. The debate on that was settled long ago. Scientific progress, technological development, industrial evolution and economic well-being are still the key objectives of all nations that aspire to greatness.

INDIA’S AWAKENING IN THE SOUTH PACIFIC IS A STRATEGIC NECESSITY, NOT A DIPLOMATIC LUXURY

Geographically distant countries have suddenly become crucial partners because they control the sea lanes that will determine the future structure of global trade and strategic stability. New Zealand, Australia and the Pacific island countries are not isolated democracies on the edge of the map any more.

EDITORIAL

A PARTNERSHIP OF TRUST, BUILT ON SHARED PURPOSE

In the midst of all these swirling currents, India and Japan have quietly become reliable partners whose cooperation is born not of anxiety but of shared aspirations. What’s unique about this partnership is the out-of-the-box balance. Japan has never tried to limit India’s foreign policy options but has always respected its strategic independence.

ELEVEN YEARS AND INDIA’S DIGITAL JOURNEY

The spread of digital connectivity has allowed those in remote areas to access financial institutions, educational platforms, healthcare services and public welfare mechanisms with a level of ease that would have once seemed improbable. Development is less a matter of physical proximity and more a matter of digital inclusion.

INDIA NEEDS TO LOOK BEYOND RUSSIAN OIL

The tightening web of Western sanctions, changing payment mechanisms, shipping limitations and insurance problems show how quickly international politics can upset commercial calculations. A discounted opportunity-based energy strategy cannot replace a resilience-based energy strategy. India must therefore adopt diversification with greater urgency.

THE REAL PRICE OF ETHANOL

If there is no option for people to choose lower ethanol blends or conventional petrol, then the marketplace is not operating based on informed consent. Public confidence grows in the soil of transparency, not coercion.

THE INCREASING NUMBER OF IPO OFFERINGS FOR SALE REQUIRES A MORE CRITICAL INVESTOR

Quick listing profits often prove too tempting to allow for a careful look at the quality of the underlying business. Discussions on social media, unofficial market premiums and aggressive promotional narratives may create an environment where the fear of missing an opportunity trumps disciplined investment analysis. History has time and again shown that too much optimism is followed by sober valuation.

Wangchuk is willing to die for the demonstrators' demand that Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan should quit.

INTERNATIONAL

INDIA NEEDS MORE THAN ELECTRICITY FOR POWER GENERATION

The manufacturing sector, digital infrastructure, transport, artificial intelligence, urban cooling, electric mobility and modern agriculture are increasingly dependent on continuous power rather than simply plenty of fuel. The future is for countries that can deliver reliable power at any hour, any day, no matter the weather and geopolitical uncertainty.

WHY INDIA MUST JOIN THE UNITED NATIONS REFUGEE CONVENTION

India’s reluctance has never been due to a lack of concern for the plight of refugees. But history also speaks of an extraordinary tradition of extending sanctuary to those fleeing persecution and conflict.

EDITORIAL

WHEN THE EROSION OF AUTHORITY BECOMES UNREVERSIBLE

the crisis the Trinamool Congress faces today appears to be of a different kind, not just because it may lose political ground, but also because the very basis of its power appears to be eroding from within. The fall of a ruling dispensation normally begins with administrative fatigue, public resentment or electoral reverses. It is unique in the current situation that the challenge is more than a standard anti-incumbency.

BREAKING NEWS

KARMA IS ABOVE EVERYTHING

It is a philosophy that requires accountability before it offers transformation. Modern society has fallen into the bad habit of attributing success to luck and adversity to circumstance. This perspective breeds helplessness and excuses people from accountability. The Buddhist perspective on karma tells a very different story.

SPRITUAL TREE

THE TRAGEDY OF INDIAN LAW AND ORDER

The upcoming trial now takes on huge constitutional importance. Filing of a chargesheet cannot be equated with proving of guilt. The hallmark of democratic societies is that every charge, no matter how emotionally charged or politically sensitive it may be, is put through the paces of an independent judiciary. Evidence must be subject to cross-examination, procedural safeguards must be complied with, and every accused person is entitled to a fair hearing.

This is the kind of episode that reminds us how fragile economies that depend almost exclusively on market mechanisms can be, without enough strategic intervention.

At the end of the day, education policy needs to inspire confidence, not fear. Before reforms of such consequence are undertaken, consultation with the States, schools, teachers and parents is not only desirable, but indispensable.

INDIA PREPARING FOR PERIOD OF CLIMATE VOLATILITY

We are now entering an era where the climate is not predictable but one of incessant volatility. Such a change requires a lot more than isolated, superficial expressions of concern.

WHEN POLITICAL PARTIES FAIL TO FOSTER PROVINCIAL STATESMEN

Each state has its own history, economic priorities, language identity, and cultural temperament. This kind of diversity needs leaders who claim real legitimacy in their own areas and who can voice local concerns without seeming to be subordinate to remote authority. The decline in the importance of provincial leadership also has an impact on governance.

POLITICS

BREAKING THE VICIOUS CYCLE OF DRUGS WITH KINDNESS AND DETERMINATION

Drug abuse has quietly emerged as one of the most serious social problems India faces. It is no longer a problem at far-away borders or organised criminal syndicates.

NEWS IN SHORTS

THE REAL MEASURE OF MILITARY SUCCESS

The human aspect of every military operation is inescapable. Behind every strategic calculation is a soldier who is ready to face extraordinary peril in the service of the Republic. They deserve recognition for their courage that is timely, unambiguous and entirely free from political calculation.

EDITORIAL

WHEN ACCOUNTABILITY IS THE REAL TEST OF FAITH

Modern governance has no shortage of mechanisms to protect public donations. Digital accounting, real-time surveillance, periodic forensic audits, transparent procurement procedures and independent oversight are now standard features across several major religious institutions worldwide. These measures do not diminish the spiritual or commercialise the faith.
